configs.cache.description: Inspect cached responses and clear segments when necessary.
configs.cache.empty: No cached responses for this tenant.
configs.cache.purgeAllConfirm: Purge all cached entries for this tenant?
configs.cache.purgeSegmentConfirm: Purge cached entries for this segment?
configs.cache.title: Cache overview
configs.moduleTelemetry.clear.confirmTex: This removes current module telemetry and local process telemetry files for this development instance.
configs.moduleTelemetry.description: Preview module-level resource attribution collected in this process.
configs.moduleTelemetry.modules.descript: Aggregated module stats from {{from}} to now across {{buckets}}. Startup and running rows are separated.
configs.moduleTelemetry.modules.empty: No module activity has been recorded for this range yet.
configs.moduleTelemetry.modules.interval: Startup is the first {{interval}} bucket after telemetry starts. Later buckets are running.
configs.moduleTelemetry.modules.title: Modules
configs.moduleTelemetry.overview.callsTo: Total tracked operation calls in the selected range.
configs.moduleTelemetry.overview.cpuTool: Total CPU time attributed to tracked module operations in the selected range.
configs.moduleTelemetry.overview.growthT: Average positive growth per hour of measured activity in the selected range: total growth divided by the hours actually covered by tracked buckets, not the full wall-clock span. Idle stretches with no tracked calls do not dilute this rate, so it stays comparable across range presets. This is allocation pressure over time, not the current live process memory.
configs.moduleTelemetry.overview.modules: Unique modules with tracked activity in the selected range. Startup and running are shown as separate rows inside each module group.
configs.moduleTelemetry.overview.title: Usage overview
configs.moduleTelemetry.range.label: Time range
configs.moduleTelemetry.table.concurrent: {{percent}}% overlapped other calls — CPU/heap/RSS less certain
configs.moduleTelemetry.table.signalsToo: Flags a module whose CPU, heap growth, RSS growth, or errors average at or above a heavy-usage threshold per 5-minute bucket it was active in during this range. This is a per-bucket average, not a raw range-wide total, so the same sustained workload looks the same regardless of the selected time range.
configs.moduleTelemetry.title: Module telemetry
configs.systemStatus.actions.purgeCacheC: Purge all cached data now? This clears cache entries across every tenant.
configs.systemStatus.categories.cachingD: Cache providers and TTL controls for backend responses.
configs.systemStatus.categories.entities: Compatibility toggles for custom entity persistence.
configs.systemStatus.categories.loggingD: Tune verbosity and SQL logging for diagnostics.
configs.systemStatus.categories.profilin: Flags that control request and query profiling outputs.
configs.systemStatus.categories.queryInd: Controls for query index maintenance and safety nets.
configs.systemStatus.categories.security: Password policy requirements enforced at login and user creation.
configs.systemStatus.description: Review debugging, cache, and logging flags that shape backend behaviour.
configs.systemStatus.details.updateHint: Update by running `export {{key}}=value` or editing `.env`, then restart the app.
configs.systemStatus.invalidResponse: Unexpected response when loading system status
configs.systemStatus.title: System status
configs.systemStatus.variables.cacheSqli: Filesystem path for the SQLite cache file.
configs.systemStatus.variables.enableCru: Enable the CRUD API response cache layer.
configs.systemStatus.variables.entitiesB: Mirror custom entity writes into the legacy EAV tables.
configs.systemStatus.variables.forceQuer: Serve query index results even when coverage is partial.
Fallback log level applied when verbosity is not set.
configs.systemStatus.variables.logVerbos: Overrides structured log verbosity such as debug or trace.
configs.systemStatus.variables.nextPubli: Browser-side flag enabling the tree profiler from the client bundle.
configs.systemStatus.variables.omCrudPro: Filters enabling detailed CRUD profiling payloads.
configs.systemStatus.variables.omProfile: Comma separated filters enabling the backend tree profiler.
configs.systemStatus.variables.omQeProfi: Filters enabling hybrid query engine profiling snapshots.
configs.systemStatus.variables.optimizeI: Skip immediate coverage recalculation during read operations.
configs.systemStatus.variables.passwordM: Minimum number of characters required for passwords.
configs.systemStatus.variables.passwordR: Require at least one numeric character.
Require at least one special character.
Require at least one uppercase letter.
configs.systemStatus.variables.queryEngi: Log raw SQL generated by the hybrid query engine.
configs.systemStatus.variables.queryInde: Number of coverage refreshes dispatched per chunk during the post-login warmup sweep.
Kill switch for the post-login coverage warmup sweep.
Delay in milliseconds added between warmup chunks to spread out peak memory/connection usage.
Minimum time in milliseconds between warmup sweeps for the same entity type and tenant.
configs.systemStatus.variables.scheduleA: Automatically schedule query index reindex jobs when gaps appear.
configs.upgrades.attachmentsOrgReconcile: Version {{version}} fixes attachments that were saved under the wrong organization. Run the reconciliation to move existing attachments to the organization of the record they belong to.
configs.upgrades.devicesPushTokenEncrypt: Version {{version}} adds mobile push and encrypts device push tokens at rest. Existing tenants have no encryption map for the new device entity, so tokens registered after the upgrade would be stored unencrypted. Run this to seed the map so push tokens are encrypted.
